summary: Loan Application Service — Create Loan Application Endpoint
description:
Implement the POST /api/v1/applications endpoint in loan-service. This covers all work needed to accept, validate, and respond to a loan application submission request: shared enums and request/response DTOs in loanflow-common, the controller method with RBAC and @Valid enforcement, the service interface, and a stub service implementation that returns a new CreateLoanApplicationResponse instance matching the API reference shape.

loanflow-common already exists and is parented by loanflow-parent, which inherits spring-boot-starter-parent. Validation and Lombok are version-managed by the parent chain. loanflow-common must declare only what a DTO library strictly needs — spring-boot-starter-validation and lombok — and must not pull in web, actuator, or resilience4j.
userType: backend developer owning the Loan Application Service
functionality: implement the create loan application endpoint with validated request DTOs, a matching response DTO, and a stubbed service layer that returns a response instance aligned with the API reference
benefit: so the POST /api/v1/applications endpoint is fully addressable, role-protected, and returns the correct documented response shape before business logic implementation begins

stories.summary: Configure loanflow-common with minimal DTO dependencies and shared domain enums
stories.description:
Trim the existing loanflow-common pom.xml to only what a DTO library requires, then add the domain enums used by the create application request and response contracts.

The current pom pulls in spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-actuator, and resilience4j, none of which belong in a shared DTO module. Every service that depends on loanflow-common unnecessarily inherits those transitive deps. Because loanflow-common is already parented by loanflow-parent (which inherits spring-boot-starter-parent), all versions are managed — no version attributes are needed in the trimmed pom.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: loanflow-common builds as a plain JAR with trimmed dependencies
acceptanceCriteria.given: the pom contains only spring-boot-starter-validation and lombok
acceptanceCriteria.when: mvnw clean package is run from backend/loanflow-common
acceptanceCriteria.then: the module compiles and produces a plain JAR without errors and no spring-boot repackage goal runs

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: loan-service does not receive web or actuator from loanflow-common transitively
acceptanceCriteria.given: the trimmed loanflow-common is a dependency of loan-service
acceptanceCriteria.when: mvn dependency:tree is run on loan-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: spring-boot-starter-web and spring-boot-starter-actuator are not listed as transitive dependencies originating from loanflow-common

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Domain enums are importable from loan-service without JPA annotations
acceptanceCriteria.given: LoanType, LoanStatus, and EmploymentStatus exist under com.loanflow.common.enums
acceptanceCriteria.when: a class in loan-service imports these enums
acceptanceCriteria.then: the imports resolve without error and no jakarta.persistence annotations are present on the enum types

tasks.summary: Trim loanflow-common pom.xml to DTO-only dependencies
tasks.description:
Edit backend/loanflow-common/pom.xml. Remove the following dependencies:

- spring-boot-starter
- spring-boot-starter-web
- spring-boot-starter-actuator
- resilience4j-core
- resilience4j-retry
- resilience4j-circuitbreaker
- jackson-databind (available transitively from spring-boot-starter-validation; no need to redeclare)

Retain or add only the following. No version attributes — all versions are managed by loanflow-parent:

- spring-boot-starter-validation (provides jakarta.validation annotations: @NotNull, @NotBlank, @Valid, etc.)
- lombok (optional, annotation processor only)

Verify the module still compiles cleanly with mvnw clean package after the change.

tasks.summary: Create shared domain enums under com.loanflow.common.enums

stories.summary: Create request DTOs for POST /api/v1/applications in loanflow-common
stories.description: Implement CreateLoanApplicationRequest and its nested ApplicantDetailsRequest in loanflow-common under com.loanflow.common.dto.application.request. Both classes carry Bean Validation annotations so the controller can apply @Valid and delegate all constraint checking to the framework. Field names, types, and constraints are derived from the API reference for POST /api/v1/applications.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: CreateLoanApplicationRequest fails validation when loanType is null
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest is built with a null loanType
acceptanceCriteria.then: a constraint violation is raised identifying the loanType field

acceptanceCriteria.given: an ApplicantDetailsRequest is built with email set to 'not-an-email'
acceptanceCriteria.then: a constraint violation is raised on the email field

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: CreateLoanApplicationRequest fails validation when amount is below minimum
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest is built with amount set to 500.00
acceptanceCriteria.then: a constraint violation is raised on the amount field referencing the minimum value

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: A fully valid CreateLoanApplicationRequest passes all constraints
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest is built with valid loanType, amount, termMonths, annualIncome, employmentStatus, and a valid applicantDetails block

stories.description: Implement CreateLoanApplicationResponse in loanflow-common under com.loanflow.common.dto.application.response. Field names and types match the API reference response shape for POST /api/v1/applications exactly so Jackson produces the documented contract without additional configuration.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: CreateLoanApplicationResponse serialises to the documented POST /api/v1/applications shape
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationResponse is built with applicationId 50021, status SUBMITTED, submittedAt, loanType PERSONAL, amount 25000, and nextStep UPLOAD_REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON contains applicationId, status, submittedAt, loanType, amount, and nextStep at the top level matching the API reference example

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: LoanStatus and LoanType fields serialise as string enum values
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationResponse is built with status LoanStatus.SUBMITTED and loanType LoanType.PERSONAL
acceptanceCriteria.then: status serialises as the string 'SUBMITTED' and loanType as 'PERSONAL'

stories.summary: Implement POST /api/v1/applications endpoint with service scaffold
stories.description:
Implement the POST /api/v1/applications endpoint in loan-service. This story creates LoanApplicationController with the single create method, the LoanApplicationService interface with the createApplication method signature, a stub LoanApplicationServiceImpl that returns a new CreateLoanApplicationResponse instance with placeholder values matching the API reference, a GlobalExceptionHandler that converts validation failures into the standard error payload, and a SecurityConfig that activates JWT resource server validation and @PreAuthorize method security.

The stub service returns a real response instance — not UnsupportedOperationException — so the endpoint is fully exercisable and the response shape can be verified before business logic is added.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications is restricted to APPLICANT role
acceptanceCriteria.given: a request is sent to POST /api/v1/applications with a JWT carrying role LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request reaches the security filter chain
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 403 Forbidden response is returned and the controller method body is not executed

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications returns 400 with standard error payload on validation failure
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest with a null loanType is submitted with a valid APPLICANT JWT
acceptanceCriteria.then: GlobalExceptionHandler returns a 400 response containing timestamp, status, error, message, and path fields

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications returns 201 with a stub CreateLoanApplicationResponse
acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid CreateLoanApplicationRequest is submitted with a valid APPLICANT JWT
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 201 Created response is returned with a body containing applicationId, status SUBMITTED, submittedAt, loanType, amount, and nextStep UPLOAD_REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS

acceptanceCriteria.given: a request is sent to POST /api/v1/applications with no Authorization header
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request reaches the security filter chain

tasks.summary: Create LoanApplicationService interface with createApplication method
Create LoanApplicationServiceImpl stub returning a placeholder response
Create GlobalExceptionHandler with standard error payload
tasks.description:
Create com.loanflow.loanservice.exception.GlobalExceptionHandler annotated with @RestControllerAdvice.

First define the error payload:

public record ApiErrorResponse(
    Instant timestamp,
    int status,
    String error,
    String message,
    String path
) {}

Handle the following exception types:

1. MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 Bad Request
   Collect all field violation messages into a comma-separated string for the message field.

2. AccessDeniedException (org.springframework.security) → 403 Forbidden

3. Exception (catch-all) → 500 Internal Server Error with message 'An unexpected error occurred'

All handlers build an ApiErrorResponse. Source the path field from HttpServletRequest.getRequestURI().

tasks.summary: Create SecurityConfig with JWT resource server and @EnableMethodSecurity
Smoke test the endpoint via dev profile
tasks.description:
Start loan-service with the dev profile and confirm:

1. Spring context loads without errors.
2. GET /actuator/health returns 200.
3. POST /api/v1/applications with no JWT returns 401.
4. POST /api/v1/applications with a LOAN_OFFICER JWT returns 403.
5. POST /api/v1/applications with an APPLICANT JWT and a null loanType returns 400 with the ApiErrorResponse payload.
6. POST /api/v1/applications with an APPLICANT JWT and a valid body returns 201 with a CreateLoanApplicationResponse containing status SUBMITTED and nextStep UPLOAD_REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.
